meta.description: Updated Automated Student Assessment Prize dataset (ASAP 2.0), released as a Kaggle competition focused on English language learning essay scoring.

summary.note: Early stage. Citations reflect ASAP 2.0's role as a go-to benchmark in the AES space. March 2026 was the first systematic sweep.

papers.title: An Early Review of Generative Language Models in Automated Writing Evaluation
papers.use_of_dataset: Reference/comparison — review paper synthesizing 29 AES studies; ASAP 2.0 likely cited as a key benchmark dataset
papers.key_takeaway: GLMs can approximate human scoring and generate rubric-aligned feedback, but fairness and validity concerns remain. ASAP 2.0 likely referenced as a foundational benchmark in the field.

papers.title: Specialists or Generalists? Multi-Agent and Single-Agent LLMs for Essay Grading
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct — used as the primary evaluation corpus for comparing single-agent vs. multi-agent LLM grading architectures
papers.key_takeaway: Multi-agent systems better identify weak essays; few-shot calibration improves QWK by ~26%. ASAP 2.0 used as the core benchmark.

papers.title: TopicENA: Enabling Epistemic Network Analysis at Scale through Automated Topic-Based Coding
papers.use_of_dataset: Likely used as a large-scale test corpus to demonstrate TopicENA's scalability
papers.key_takeaway: Moderate connection. ASAP 2.0 used to validate scalability of BERTopic-based ENA pipeline; not an AES-focused use but demonstrates dataset utility beyond scoring tasks.

papers.title: Benchmarking Educational LLMs with Analytics: A Case Study on Gender Bias in Feedback
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct — 600 essays drawn from the ASAP 2.0 Kaggle dataset used as primary corpus for gender bias experiments
papers.key_takeaway: Strong connection. ASAP 2.0 used to investigate whether LLMs produce systematically different feedback based on implicit and explicit gender cues in student writing. UCL and CMU authorship adds institutional weight.

papers.title: Multilingual Embedding Probes Fail to Generalize Across Learner Corpora
papers.abstract: Trains probes on Qwen3-Embedding models to predict CEFR proficiency levels across nine learner corpora in seven languages. Probes perform well within training distribution but collapse cross-corpus, suggesting models capture corpus-specific properties rather than abstract proficiency.
papers.use_of_dataset: Direct — ASAP 2.0 is one of nine learner corpora in the cross-corpus evaluation
